mechanism_narrative: A sustained upward trend in FEDFUNDS over the trailing six months signals progressive tightening of financial conditions, raising the discount rate applied to future equity cash flows and compressing valuation multiples, which produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS rose from near-zero in 2015 to 1.82% by June 2018 amid ongoing Fed hiking cycle
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC for FEDFUNDS trend remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 60-day forward returns during rising-rate periods are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When UNRATE falls below 4.0%, indicating a historically tight labor market, wage pressure and inflation risk increase, prompting investors to anticipate accelerated monetary tightening and reduce equity risk exposure, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE recorded at 4.0% in June 2018, near the lowest level since 2000
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with tight labor market
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day forward returns in more than 60% of instances when UNRATE < 4.0%, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window under this trigger.

mechanism_narrative: When FEDFUNDS is rising simultaneously with accelerating CPIAUCSL year-over-year growth, the joint signal of tightening policy plus persistent inflation raises expectations of faster future rate hikes beyond current guidance, causing an abrupt repricing of equity risk premia and negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL vintage value 250.857 in June 2018 reflecting inflation near or above Fed's 2% target
regime_hint: monetary tightening cycle with rising inflation
failure_condition: Joint-trigger episodes show no statistically significant difference in 5-day forward returns versus non-triggered periods (p > 0.05), or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: During late-cycle expansion, sustained month-over-month growth in RSAFS reflects robust consumer spending that supports corporate earnings expectations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days, but this relationship weakens or reverses once the expansion matures into a slowdown phase.
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS vintage value 501971 million in May 2018 indicating continued retail sales growth
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with strong consumer demand
failure_condition: RSAFS growth trend fails to precede positive 20-day returns in more than 55% of late-cycle expansion instances, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window within this regime.

mechanism_narrative: A persistent upward trend in INDPRO signals expanding industrial output and corporate production capacity utilization, which supports higher forward earnings growth expectations and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO vintage value 107.3006 in May 2018 continuing an upward multi-year trend
failure_condition: Rank IC for INDPRO trend remains below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window, or 60-day forward returns during INDPRO uptrends are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A decline in HOUST of more than 10% from its trailing 12-month peak signals weakening housing demand, an early indicator of broader consumer and construction-sector slowdown, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days as markets price in decelerating growth.
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST vintage value 1350 thousand units in May 2018, below prior cyclical peaks near 1350-1350 range with volatility
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with slowing housing sector
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day forward returns in more than 60% of triggered HOUST decline instances, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window under this trigger.

mechanism_narrative: When PAYEMS month-over-month growth exceeds its trailing 12-month average by more than one standard deviation, it signals unexpectedly strong labor demand that boosts near-term risk appetite, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS MoM growth > trailing 12-month average + 1 std dev
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS vintage value 148912 thousand persons in June 2018 continuing steady payroll expansion
failure_condition: Triggered PAYEMS surprises fail to produce positive 5-day forward returns in more than 55% of instances, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous deceleration in GDPC1 quarter-over-quarter growth and INDPRO month-over-month growth signals a synchronized weakening of aggregate demand and industrial supply capacity, an early recession warning that produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days; neither series alone reliably captures this turning point.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 QoQ growth decelerates for 2 consecutive quarters AND INDPRO MoM growth turns negative
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 vintage value 17371.854 billion in Q1 2018 subject to high revision risk
failure_condition: Joint-trigger episodes fail to precede negative 60-day forward returns in more than 55% of instances, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window under this joint trigger.
